commit.message:
Add @ItemType for InstallMethod docs (#342)

Allow declaration comments to override the manual item kind for
InstallMethod entries via @ItemType using the GAP and GAPDoc
abbreviations Func, Oper, Attr, and Prop. Document InstallMethod
support in declaration comments and add regression coverage for
the new override and invalid values.

fixtures and expected output.

commit.message:
fix(release): 发布0.33.1并修复WebUI内嵌启动链路

- 将 webui_runtime 的 API/WebUI 启动从外部 shell 子进程切换为插件内嵌 uvicorn 托管，补齐关键路径可观测日志。
- 新增 infrastructure/webui_runtime_server.py：统一管理运行时启动/停止，WebUI 直接托管 data/static/webui 并内置 /api 反向代理。
- 删除已废弃生产脚本 scripts/run_api_prod.sh 与 scripts/run_webui_prod.sh，并同步 README 与运行时端口契约测试。
- 增加构造阶段 runtime bootstrap 与后台任务取消逻辑，降低 enable_webui 打开后的生命周期漏触发风险。
- 更新版本声明到 0.33.1（main.py、metadata.yaml）并补充 CHANGELOG。
- 回归：bash scripts/run_regression.sh（537 passed）。

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): add pre-production disclaimer

Summary:
- add a short disclaimer near the top of the README that the repo
  is not production-ready yet
- mention the interest in developing the project into a bachelor’s
  thesis and invite relevant contacts to reach out

Rationale:
- set the right expectation for readers before they evaluate the
  project for real deployments
- make the community outreach goal visible in the main project
  entry point

Validation:
- reviewed README.md rendering in source form

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Improve break-loop stack traces (#6257)

This reworks break-loop stack traces so the initial traceback and
later `Where()` output are driven by the same visible traceback
state.

The break loop now tracks both the real environment used for local
variable lookup and the first visible traceback frame. That makes
ordinary errors, kernel-thrown errors, and no-method-found errors
behave more consistently: `Where()` now shows the same top visible
frame that was shown on entry, while method-not-found still keeps
its helper environment available for `ShowArguments`,
`ShowDetails`, and `ShowMethods`.

This also adds `CurrentEnv()` for inspecting the currently
selected break-loop environment and updates the break-loop docs
and tests accordingly.

On top of that, visible stack frames are now rendered with
numbered prefixes in the style `*[1]`, ` [2]`, ` [3]`, where the
`*` denotes the active frame. For me, that makes it *much* easier
to use `DownEnv`/`UpEnv` correctly, and also increases readability
of in longer traces. The manual examples and special REPL
transcripts were refreshed to match the new output.

AI disclosure: prepared with help from Codex for implementation,
tests, and documentation.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(postgres): document data-copy migration workflow

Summary:
- add explicit guidance for drafting production migration SQL from
  dev or local Postgres rows
- document checks for source data, target defaults, and foreign-key
  handling before writing copied data into a pending migration
- clarify that generated primary keys should not be copied directly
  and dependent inserts should resolve IDs in SQL

Rationale:
- make the Postgres skill safer when users want to promote selected
  dev data into production-facing migration files
- capture the expected adaptation work so copied values are treated
  as a draft instead of assumed production-ready data

Tests:
- not run (docs-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(simulator): use a struct for runtime state

Summary:
- add `EtherCAT.Simulator.State` as the internal runtime state
  struct
- update the simulator GenServer to initialize and update typed
  state instead of ad hoc maps
- move fault read-model conversion into snapshot rendering so the
  runtime state shape stays consistent

Rationale:
- the simulator state shape is fixed and should use a struct for
  stronger invariants and clearer internal ownership
- keeping runtime state typed avoids accidental loss of the struct
  when applying updates during fault clearing and snapshot reads

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/ring_test.exs --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(worktree): migrate daemon identity and cwd

Move daemon-facing worktree call sites to project-root identity,
validate dispatch cwd ownership, and cover the worktree dispatch
contract with CLI and daemon regressions.

Task: @01KKCEE31YMBRNYTF7G1VVR9GH
Spec: @worktree-support
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(simulator): simplify runtime to singleton

Summary:
- route simulator and UDP lifecycle through a single named runtime
  and supervisor
- remove unused per-server simulator and slave API variants and
  update docs and ring integration coverage to match
- add simulator lifecycle regression tests for start and stop
  cleanup paths

Rationale:
- the codebase uses a single simulator instance, so carrying
  multi-runtime plumbing added shutdown bugs and unnecessary API
  complexity
- a singleton runtime makes UDP metadata, signal wiring, and test
  cleanup deterministic for integration runs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/logical_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/process_image_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/al_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/esc_image_test.exs --max-failures 1
- timeout 120s mix test test/integration/simulator/ring_test.exs
  --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(session): add text search across daemon and web UI

Extend session text search with a daemon search endpoint, URL-backed /sessions search mode, and shared metadata prefiltering before events.jsonl scans. Also add CLI support for --status in search and cover the new behavior in search tests.

Task: @01KKB3PKZYH6VME39Q0H0TRTQ0

Spec: @session-text-search

commit.message:
Upgrade BotKit to Fedify 2.0

Align BotKit with Fedify 2.0.3 and its modular package split.
Move vocab and runtime imports to @fedify/vocab,
@fedify/vocab-runtime, and @fedify/denokv.

Update the public API to match Fedify 2.0 by replacing
LanguageTag-based surfaces with Intl.Locale, using plain strings
for software.version, and removing parseSemVer(), SemVer,
LanguageTag, and parseLanguageTag() exports.

Refresh examples, docs, and CHANGES.md to reflect the new
package paths and API changes. Update lockfiles and manifests
for the new dependency graph.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: botkit
repository.description: A framework for creating ActivityPub bots

commit.message:
fix(core): backport upstream orchestration and workflow fixes

Summary:
- backport symlink-safe workspace and Codex cwd validation using
  canonical path checks
- ignore superseded poll and retry timers, and stop running issues
  that disappear from refresh results
- align workflow state parsing and SPEC.md with upstream list-only
  tracker states and lowercase-only state normalization
- add regression coverage for path safety, sandbox passthrough,
  retry tokening, missing running issues, and workflow parsing

Rationale:
- upstream Symphony fixed correctness gaps around workspace safety,
  timer coalescing, and refresh reconciliation that we still had
- our workflow contract needs to match the current upstream spec so
  the parser and documentation do not drift
- locking the changes down with targeted tests reduces the chance of
  reintroducing these failures later

Tests:
- npm run check
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

simplify pipeline call flow

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Task: @01KKCYMCNZ94ZJKP84XYCCBVNM
Spec: @plan-export
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(core): reduce noisy test output

Summary:
- add a root logger console toggle so tests can suppress runtime log
  chatter without changing file logging behavior
- preload a quiet test setup and switch the Node test runner to the
  dot reporter for compact agent-friendly output
- cover the new logger toggle with a unit test

Rationale:
- integration tests were emitting orchestrator and agent runner logs on
  every passing run, which wastes context during coding-agent sessions
- keeping logs opt-in preserves a debugging escape hatch without making
  normal test runs noisy

Tests:
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(core): add hierarchy-aware issue orchestration

Summary:
- load parent and child hierarchy from Linear and expose it in workflow
  templates and runner prompt rendering
- block Todo parent dispatch while child work or blockers remain active
  and allow ready sibling issues to run in parallel
- persist service logs to tmp/symphony.log, preserve websocket shutdown
  details, and treat post-turn session closes as clean boundaries
- refresh workflow docs, remove old workflow examples, and update the
  Linear skill guidance for GitHub PR auto-linking

Rationale:
- agents need parent and sibling context to execute parent and child
  issues without extra manual tracker inspection
- dispatch should respect hierarchy and dependency state so higher-level
  work does not start before prerequisite child work is terminal
- richer logging and transport detail improve observability for
  unattended runs and align the docs with the current workflow

Tests:
- npm test --workspace @symphony/core
- npm run typecheck --workspace @symphony/core

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(core): harden logger and app-server shutdown

Summary:
- make persistent logger setup best-effort so CLI startup does not fail
  when the default log path cannot be created
- suppress transport-close warnings during intentional app-server
  shutdown and reject in-flight work directly from stop()
- add regression coverage for unwritable log paths and intentional
  session teardown

Rationale:
- persistent logging is optional, so an unwritable cwd should degrade to
  console logging instead of preventing service startup
- successful runs were emitting warning-level transport failures during
  normal teardown, which obscured real transport problems

Tests:
- npm test --workspace @symphony/core
- npm run typecheck --workspace @symphony/core

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: resolve worktree project roots

Add worktree-aware project root resolution and thread projectRoot through context, daemon identity, and serve/shadow defaults.

repository.name: claude-codex-pair-loop
repository.description: Shell tools for running iterative pair-programming loops between Claude Code and OpenAI Codex, with standard and MCP-enabled modes.

commit.message:
let CLI.run own process exit

Treat the CLI entry routine as a true command entrypoint: help and version exit directly, parse failures exit 1, and normal completion falls through with implicit success.

Update exe/jrf to call Jrf::CLI.run without wrapping it in exit, which removes the need for the local parser exit sentinel.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
inline the remaining OptionParser logic

Keep the parser setup linear inside CLI.run by using an explicit sentinel for help/version exits and validating --atomic-write-bytes inline instead of extracting a helper.

This leaves the fixed help text intact while matching the simpler structure requested in review.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
refactor(simulator): streamline runtime and ring integration

Summary:
- add a simpler simulator lifecycle with `start/1` and unified
  `stop/1` handling for in-memory and UDP-backed runtimes
- replace the old simulator deep test with ring-shaped integration tests
  for both simulator and real hardware using EK1100, EL1809, and EL2809
- narrow the public simulator slave API toward driver-backed devices and
  align docs/tests with the new ring and hardware-first model

Rationale:
- the previous simulator setup exposed too much internal structure and
  required awkward manual endpoint cleanup in tests
- ring-shaped integration coverage better matches the actual target
  topology and keeps simulator and hardware validation aligned
- driver-backed simulation is the clearer long-term public API than
  profile-style convenience builders

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

simplify the OptionParser wiring

Restore the fixed help text output, keep the option definitions inline in CLI.run, and avoid routing parser results through callback lambdas.

This preserves the CLI behavior from the earlier PR while matching the simpler structure requested in review.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
switch the CLI to OptionParser

Replace the ad hoc option loop with Ruby stdlib OptionParser, preserving front-of-command option parsing while adding conventional -V/--version support alongside -v/--verbose.

Keep the existing help content shape, retain --no-jit and atomic-write parsing, and add executable-level coverage for help/version behavior.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
add a --no-jit CLI option

Enable YJIT after option parsing by default, but allow callers to opt out with --no-jit when they want deterministic non-JIT startup.

Update help text and add executable-level tests covering default enablement and the opt-out path.
